channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: One Nation’s Influence Grows Amid Shifts in Political Support
item.description: In recent months, the political landscape in Australia has seen the resurgence of One Nation, with Pauline Hanson becoming a more prominent and popular political figure. Last year, many observers cons...

item.title: Newfoundland Teens Travel to Europe to Honour Hockey History and Remembrance
item.description: A group of teenagers from Newfoundland and Labrador are embarking on a unique journey to Europe, blending their love of hockey with a meaningful historical experience. The teens will visit several Eur...

item.title: President Museveni’s Granddaughter Sasii Marries Following Traditional Dowry Ceremony
item.description: Sasii, the granddaughter of Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni, has tied the knot in a beautiful and elegant wedding just days after completing her traditional dowry rites. The bride, officially known ...

item.title: Ethnocentrique completes FFP pilot phase, creates over 10,000 jobs in Aba
item.description: Ethnocentrique don wrap up the second edition of The Fashion Games, marking the end of the pilot phase of the Fashion Future Programme (FFP) wey dem dey run with Mastercard Foundation. Dis programme d...

item.title: Two women die in failed small boat attempt to cross English Channel from France
item.description: Two women lost their lives during an attempt to cross the English Channel from northern France to the United Kingdom in a small makeshift boat. The incident occurred off the coast near Neufchâtel-Hard...

item.title: CAAP issues advisory on flight safety as Mayon Volcano erupts
item.description: The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) issued a flight safety advisory following the eruption of Mayon Volcano in Legazpi, Albay on May 2, 2026. The advisory urges airline operators, p...

item.title: Sindh Government Clarifies Progress and Challenges on BRT Red Line Project
item.description: Sindh Senior Minister Sharjeel Inam Memon has acknowledged that the BRT Red Line project in Karachi is quite challenging, but he insisted that the provincial government has not caused any delays. He e...

item.title: Four Die and Several Injured in Narok-Bomet Highway Accident
item.description: Narok County witnessed a tragic accident near Ololulunga/Katakala bridge along the Narok–Bomet highway, where four people lost their lives and several others were injured. According to Narok Police Co...

item.title: Global Demand for Chinese Renewable Technologies Spikes Amid Oil Supply Disruptions
item.description: Following the outbreak of the war in Iran, countries facing oil shortages have increasingly turned to renewable energy solutions, with China emerging as a key global supplier. According to the energy ...

item.title: Nigeria summons South African envoy to address attacks on Nigerians during protests
item.description: The Nigerian government don take diplomatic step as dem summon South Africa High Commissioner following recent xenophobic attacks against Nigerians and other African migrants. According to statement w...

item.title: Suspended officials in South Africa cost taxpayers R800m annually
item.description: South African taxpayers are spending an alarming R800 million annually on the salaries of over 670 public servants suspended from national and provincial departments. Many of these officials have been...

item.title: List of Declared Special Non-Working Days in Philippine Localities for May 2026
item.description: This article compiles the officially declared special non-working days across different provinces, cities, and municipalities in the Philippines for May 2026. These declarations are based on proclamat...

item.title: Nigerians in South Africa Face Violence and Xenophobia
item.description: This article na heartfelt message from Femi Fani-Kayode to Nigerians wey dey South Africa. E draw parallel between the betrayal of Julius Caesar by Brutus and how some Nigerians dey suffer xenophobic ...

item.title: GameStop Builds Stake in eBay Ahead of Potential Acquisition Bid
item.description: Shares of eBay surged approximately 10% in after-hours trading on Friday, May 1, 2026, following a Wall Street Journal report that GameStop has been quietly accumulating a stake in the online resale p...

item.title: Plaid Cymru and Reform UK Lead the Race Ahead of Welsh Senedd Elections
item.description: The upcoming Welsh Senedd elections are shaping up to be a contest primarily between Plaid Cymru and Reform UK, signalling a potential end to nearly three decades of Labour dominance. Plaid Cymru lead...

item.title: Lady Gabriella Windsor moves out of London flat two years after husband’s death
item.description: Lady Gabriella Windsor, daughter of Prince and Princess Michael of Kent, has moved out of her London flat more than two years after the death of her husband, Thomas Kingston. The 45-year-old royal was...

item.title: Nato holds discussions with film and TV professionals amid debate over messaging influence
item.description: Nato has conducted a series of closed-door meetings with film and television writers, directors, and producers in Los Angeles, Brussels, and Paris, with a fourth session scheduled in London. The meeti...

item.title: Government must address inefficiencies in disciplinary processes to save public funds
item.description: South Africans are increasingly frustrated by the delays in government disciplinary processes, especially concerning officials on suspension while facing allegations of misconduct. A report has reveal...

item.title: Exploring Kyushu: Japan’s Historic Island Balancing Tradition and Tourism
item.description: Kyushu, the southernmost of Japan's major islands, offers a unique glimpse into a Japan beyond the well-trodden Golden Route of Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka. While these urban centres face rising tension w...

item.title: Authorities Conduct DNA Tests to Confirm Identity of Human Remains Found in Komati River Crocodile
item.description: Police in Mpumalanga are awaiting DNA test results to confirm whether human remains discovered inside a crocodile in the Komati River belong to a missing Komatipoort businessman. The businessman had b...
